skill.name: legal-advisor
skill.description: Drafts technology-focused legal document templates, including privacy policies, terms of service, cookie policies, disclaimers, and data processing agreements.
skill.summary: Create structured legal document templates for privacy, compliance, and software business needs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the only scanned file, SKILL.md. The backticks at line 26 mark an inline file path, line 58 names the ePrivacy Directive, and line 3 is description text with no weak cryptography. No malicious intent, data access, command execution, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown File Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 26. The line uses Markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Markdown inline code span containing a file path. There is no shell syntax, script block, or executable instruction on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cookie Regulation Mention
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged browser credential files at SKILL.md line 58. The line only lists the ePrivacy Directive for cookies and does not reference browser storage or credential files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a plain regulatory reference. No evidence found of file access, browser profile paths, credential names, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Legal Description Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm usage at SKILL.md line 3. The line describes drafting legal notices and contains no cryptographic function or algorithm reference.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is YAML front matter for the skill description. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, cipher use, hashing code, or security implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear legal templates for privacy, terms, cookies, and compliance notices. This skill creates structured drafts and review checklists for attorney validation.
actual_capabilities: Drafts privacy policies for common technology products and data practices.
Creates terms of service, disclaimers, and software licensing sections.
Builds cookie policy language and consent implementation notes.
Produces data processing agreement templates with placeholder fields.
Lists jurisdiction-specific considerations for GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, and related rules.
Adds compliance checklists and attorney review flags.
limitations: Does not provide binding legal advice or replace a qualified attorney.
Requires accurate business details, jurisdictions, and data processing inputs.
May not reflect legal changes after the model or skill content was created.
Cannot verify final enforceability or regulatory acceptance.

use_cases.title: Launch a SaaS Product
use_cases.description: Create first drafts of privacy, terms, cookies, and subscription clauses before attorney review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Updates
use_cases.description: Compare product data practices against GDPR, CCPA, and cookie notice requirements.

use_cases.title: Document Data Processing
use_cases.description: Draft processor, subprocessor, retention, and security language for vendor agreements.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Privacy Policy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard website or app policy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a privacy policy for a [product type] serving users in [jurisdictions]. We collect [data types] for [purposes]. Include placeholders for company details.

prompt_templates.title: Create Terms of Service
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for customer-facing product terms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create terms of service for a [business model] software product. Cover accounts, acceptable use, subscriptions, termination, liability, intellectual property, and dispute handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Cookie Compliance
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for consent and tracking language.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a cookie policy and consent checklist for [website or app]. We use [analytics, advertising, functional cookies]. Address [target jurisdictions].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Processing Agreement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for business-to-business privacy contracting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a data processing agreement for [controller] and [processor]. Include processing instructions, subprocessors, security measures, breach notices, audits, transfers, and deletion terms.

output_examples.input: Draft a privacy policy for a fitness app that collects email, workout logs, and payment data.
output_examples.output: A structured privacy policy with sections for data collected, purposes, sharing, retention, user rights, payments, contact details, and attorney review notes.

output_examples.input: Create terms of service for a subscription project management tool.
output_examples.output: A terms draft covering accounts, subscriptions, billing, acceptable use, customer content, service changes, termination, disclaimers, liability, and governing law placeholders.

output_examples.input: Prepare a cookie notice for an e-commerce site using analytics and remarketing tools.
output_examples.output: A cookie policy draft with cookie categories, consent language, opt-out guidance, retention notes, and an implementation checklist.

best_practices: Provide jurisdictions, product type, user groups, data categories, and third-party vendors.
Use the drafts as starting points and have counsel review final language.
Update documents when data practices, vendors, or applicable laws change.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated legal text without qualified legal review.
Do not omit sensitive data categories, children, advertising, or international transfers.
Do not reuse another company's legal terms without adapting them to your practices.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a lawyer?
faq.answer: No. It creates legal document templates and checklists for review by a qualified attorney.

faq.question: Which documents can it draft?
faq.answer: It can draft privacy policies, terms of service, cookie policies, disclaimers, and data processing agreements.

faq.question: Does it support GDPR and CCPA language?
faq.answer: Yes. It can include GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, LGPD, COPPA, and related compliance sections.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide jurisdictions, company details, product features, data categories, vendors, retention periods, and user rights workflows.

faq.question: Can it review existing legal documents?
faq.answer: It can summarize gaps and suggest checklist items, but final review should be completed by counsel.

faq.question: Is the output ready to publish?
faq.answer: No. The output is a draft template and should be validated against current law and business practices.
